file_insights.purpose: Django management command for seeding roles and permissions
key_features: Idempotent design with force and dry-run options
8 system roles across PLATFORM and ORGANIZATION scopes
35+ resource types with granular permission actions

patterns_discovered.description: Command is designed to be run multiple times safely using update_or_create with force flag to control update behavior
patterns_discovered.implementation: Uses Django ORM's update_or_create method with conditional logic for force updates

patterns_discovered.description: Implements dry-run capability using DryRunException to rollback atomic transactions without actual changes
patterns_discovered.implementation: Raises DryRunException within atomic block to trigger rollback for dry-run mode

patterns_discovered.description: Permissions are defined as combinations of resources and actions rather than discrete permission objects
patterns_discovered.implementation: Nested dictionary mapping resources to applicable actions with code generation

patterns_discovered.description: Roles are segregated by scope (PLATFORM vs ORGANIZATION) to enable hierarchical access control
patterns_discovered.implementation: RoleScope enum used to differentiate platform-level and organization-level roles

patterns_discovered.description: All database operations wrapped in atomic transaction for consistency

gotchas_discovered.description: DryRunException is raised but never defined or imported in the visible code
gotchas_discovered.impact: Command will crash when --dry-run flag is used

gotchas_discovered.description: Permission code uses '{resource}.{action}' format but no validation prevents duplicate codes if resource names are not unique
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential permission collision if resource naming isn't carefully controlled

gotchas_discovered.description: The permission update logic sets all permission_data only if created=True, but defaults are used in update_or_create, which may not fully update existing records
gotchas_discovered.impact: Force flag may not properly update all fields when record exists

gotchas_discovered.description: Git diff output is truncated with '... (truncated, 26733 chars total)' indicating incomplete view of full implementation
gotchas_discovered.impact: Cannot verify complete role permissions and relationship mappings

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created Django management command infrastructure for seeding roles and permissions
deliverables: Created management command package structure (2 __init__.py files)
Implemented comprehensive seed_roles command with 657 lines
Defined 35+ resources with granular permission actions
Implemented idempotent operations with force flag
Added dry-run capability for safe testing
Used atomic transactions for data consistency
critical_issues: DryRunException class undefined - will cause runtime error
approach_outcome.documentation_quality: Excellent - comprehensive docstrings and inline comments

recommendations.recommendation: Define or import DryRunException class before use
recommendations.rationale: Current code will fail at runtime when --dry-run flag is used

recommendations.recommendation: Verify complete role-permission assignment logic in truncated section
recommendations.rationale: Cannot assess if all role-permission mappings are correctly implemented

recommendations.recommendation: Create unit tests for seed_roles command covering: normal run, force update, dry-run, and error scenarios
recommendations.rationale: Complex command with multiple execution paths requires comprehensive test coverage

recommendations.recommendation: Extract permission definition dictionary to separate configuration module
recommendations.rationale: Large nested dictionary reduces readability and makes maintenance difficult

recommendations.recommendation: Add logging/output for created vs updated vs skipped records at each stage
recommendations.rationale: Current output may not clearly indicate what changed during command execution

recommendations.recommendation: Create example usage documentation showing different command invocation patterns
recommendations.rationale: Command supports multiple modes (normal, force, dry-run) that would benefit from examples
